label: Default tone dinner transition
text: Please come down and wash your hands. It's dinner time.
sourceTranslation: Dinner is almost ready. The sink is open for hands.
reviewFocus: Stays grounded in the same moment
Warm changes do not become sweeter or more parent-centered
Shorter and playful versions still feel natural

outputs.label: Shorter
outputs.prompt:
Refine one existing declarative suggestion into exactly 2 new declarative rewrites.

Original caregiver request: "Please come down and wash your hands. It's dinner time."
Selected source suggestion: "Dinner is almost ready. The sink is open for hands."
Tone: Use a neutral, warm, observational tone with simple everyday wording.
Variation direction: "Shorter". Make both rewrites tighter and more concise than the source suggestion while preserving every important part of the original caregiver request. Do not become clipped, abrupt, or bossy.


Requirements:
- Preserve the full meaning of the original caregiver request.
- Stay anchored to the selected source suggestion rather than inventing a totally new angle.
- Keep the same low-pressure spirit and the same general tone family as the source suggestion.
- Make the 2 rewrites meaningfully different from each other in opening words and sentence shape, not just tiny wording swaps.
- Keep both rewrites authentic and useful in a real caregiver moment.
- Do not drop important parts of the request.
- Do not become more manipulative, more praising, more performative, or more emotionally loaded.
- Do not use generic fallback phrasing or near-duplicates of the source suggestion.

Return only the valid JSON array.

label: Straightforward school transition
text: Get your backpack and get your shoes on so we can get ready for school
sourceTranslation: Shoes and backpack are both part of getting out the door for school.
reviewFocus: More straightforward does not become bossy
Shorter versions keep both tasks
Playful remains restrained

outputs.label: Shorter
outputs.prompt:
Refine one existing declarative suggestion into exactly 2 new declarative rewrites.

Original caregiver request: "Get your backpack and get your shoes on so we can get ready for school"
Selected source suggestion: "Shoes and backpack are both part of getting out the door for school."
Tone: Use a "Straightforward" tone: calm, plainspoken, and to the point with a low emotional temperature. Prefer concise observation-first phrasing and gentle availability cues over questions. Across the 3-4 suggestions, include 1-2 concise options and at least 1 slightly fuller option. Use simple everyday wording and keep the suggestions varied. Avoid jokes, hype, slang, roleplay, exaggerated metaphors, faux-choice pressure, and clipped or bossy phrasing.
Variation direction: "Shorter". Make both rewrites tighter and more concise than the source suggestion while preserving every important part of the original caregiver request. Do not become clipped, abrupt, or bossy.


Requirements:
- Preserve the full meaning of the original caregiver request.
- Stay anchored to the selected source suggestion rather than inventing a totally new angle.
- Keep the same low-pressure spirit and the same general tone family as the source suggestion.
- Make the 2 rewrites meaningfully different from each other in opening words and sentence shape, not just tiny wording swaps.
- Keep both rewrites authentic and useful in a real caregiver moment.
- Do not drop important parts of the request.
- Do not become more manipulative, more praising, more performative, or more emotionally loaded.
- Do not use generic fallback phrasing or near-duplicates of the source suggestion.

Return only the valid JSON array.

label: Equalizing multi-part bedtime
text: Please come to the bathroom, get your pajamas on, and bring your book to bed.
sourceTranslation: The bathroom, pajamas, and book all seem to be looking for a good bedtime order.
reviewFocus: Equalizing stays natural and not performative

outputs.label: Shorter
outputs.prompt:
Refine one existing declarative suggestion into exactly 2 new declarative rewrites.

Original caregiver request: "Please come to the bathroom, get your pajamas on, and bring your book to bed."
Selected source suggestion: "The bathroom, pajamas, and book all seem to be looking for a good bedtime order."
Tone: Use an "Equalizing" tone. Frame the situation as collaborative or as if the child has useful perspective. If the adult needs help or a second opinion, keep it warm and light, not mocking, helpless, or performatively foolish. Preserve dignity and felt safety.
Variation direction: "Shorter". Make both rewrites tighter and more concise than the source suggestion while preserving every important part of the original caregiver request. Do not become clipped, abrupt, or bossy.

- Keep the same low-pressure spirit and the same general tone family as the source suggestion.

- Keep both rewrites authentic and useful in a real caregiver moment.
- Do not drop important parts of the request.
- Do not become more manipulative, more praising, more performative, or more emotionally loaded.
- Do not use generic fallback phrasing or near-duplicates of the source suggestion.

label: Fewer Words lunch transition

outputs.label: Longer
outputs.prompt:

Original caregiver request: "Wash your hands and come to the kitchen for lunch."
Selected source suggestion: "Hands first, then the kitchen for lunch."
Tone: Use a "Straightforward" tone: calm, plainspoken, and to the point with a low emotional temperature. Prefer concise observation-first phrasing and gentle availability cues over questions. Across the 3-4 suggestions, include 1-2 concise options and at least 1 slightly fuller option. Use simple everyday wording and keep the suggestions varied. Avoid jokes, hype, slang, roleplay, exaggerated metaphors, faux-choice pressure, and clipped or bossy phrasing.
Variation direction: "Longer". Make both rewrites a little fuller and smoother than the source suggestion. Add only enough context or connective tissue to improve flow. Do not add new demands, emotional pressure, or invented details.
Respect the existing "Fewer Words" preference unless the chosen variation direction is "Longer", in which case slightly fuller wording is allowed while staying compact.

Variation direction: "More playful". Make both rewrites a little lighter in rhythm or wording than the source suggestion while staying grounded and easy to say out loud. Do not turn them into jokes, gimmicks, or full humorous roleplay unless the selected tone already supports that level of play.


Requirements:
- Preserve the full meaning of the original caregiver request.

- Make the 2 rewrites meaningfully different from each other in opening words and sentence shape, not just tiny wording swaps.
- Keep both rewrites authentic and useful in a real caregiver moment.

- Do not become more manipulative, more praising, more performative, or more emotionally loaded.
- Do not use generic fallback phrasing or near-duplicates of the source suggestion.

Return only the valid JSON array.

translations.translation: I wonder what fun adventures the plate, clean hands, and the couch will get up to next.
